name: Ned Land
description: Ned Land, the Canadian harpooner, is rendered in lush watercolors with a palette leaning towards warm browns, ochres, and deep blues, reflecting his ruggedness and connection to the sea. His face is a study in weathered strength, with skin tanned to a deep bronze, crisscrossed by a network of fine lines etched by years spent under the sun and wind. A thick, unruly beard of copper-red sprouts from his chin, framing a wide, resolute mouth and slightly chipped front teeth, hinting at past brawls. His nose is broad and strong, a roadmap of healed breaks and sun-kissed freckles, sitting above piercing blue eyes that gleam with an almost feral intensity, their irises speckled with gold like fragments of shattered sunlight. He favors practical, if slightly disheveled, attire: a dark, indigo-dyed wool peacoat, perpetually unbuttoned, reveals a faded ochre flannel shirt underneath. Leather breeches, stained with saltwater and grease, are tucked into knee-high, brown leather boots, showing signs of hard wear and a perpetually worn-in feel. A tattered, knitted brown cap, its brim pulled low, completes the ensemble, shadowing his brow and adding to his air of untamed, untamed grit.
